title: Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Landmark Social Media Addiction Trial
summary: A jury found the companies harmed a young user with design features that were addictive and led to her mental health distress.

title: Cheap Drones Remain Wild Card in Iran war
summary: Stopping Iran’s production of drones is critical to opening the Strait of Hormuz and halting its attacks on Gulf nations. But can it be done?

title: U.S. and Iran Are Talking About Ways to End the War: What to Know
summary: The United States is discussing ways to end hostilities with Iran, even as President Trump continues to threaten attacks and as the Pentagon dispatches more troops.

title: Air Canada C.E.O. Draws Scorn for Delivering Condolences Only in English
summary: The lack of French in Michael Rousseau’s speech about the deadly collision at LaGuardia Airport reignited a debate over linguistic inclusivity in Canada.

title: Supreme Court Sides With Internet Provider in Copyright Fight Over Pirated Music
summary: Leading music labels sued Cox Communications for failing to terminate accounts of subscribers flagged for distributing copyrighted music.

title: The 7 Biggest Decluttering Myths
summary: Experts said these were the most commonly held beliefs about organization — and they’re holding you back.

title: A Day in the Life of a New York City Junklugger
summary: The work force behind Junkluggers, a company that tosses, donates and resells what we leave behind, bears witness to grief and lives in transition.

title: Senate Housing Bill Sparks Debate About Who Gets to Own Single-Family Homes
summary: Within the Senate’s housing bill lie the terms of an unusual debate: Who gets to own — and live in — single-family homes?

title: Heirs of Dave the Potter, the Enslaved Artist, Are Battling to Recover His Legacy
summary: The descendants of David Drake learned who he was 10 years ago. They see his jars as his artistic and spiritual inheritance — and their own.

title: Ukraine Finally Got Battlefield Momentum. Now Comes a Russian Offensive.
summary: Moscow’s forces are intensifying their attacks in southern Ukraine after Kyiv made rare gains along the front.

title: An American in Russia Is Linked to Neo-Nazi Terror Cells Across Europe
summary: F.B.I. agents thought they had weakened an online hate group known as the Base. A string of European terrorism cases indicates it has resurged.

title: Why This City’s Reckoning With Cesar Chavez Is So Complicated
summary: San Jose, Calif., is where Mr. Chavez began organizing in the 1950s, and he once lived there. After revelations of sexual abuse by the labor leader, the city and his old neighborhood are confronting his legacy.

title: A Critical Political Season Could Decide if Alaska Is a Failed ‘Petrostate’
summary: A governor who spent two terms cutting services to preserve Alaskans’ oil-funded annual checks is leaving office. Voters must now decide what comes next for the state’s faltering fiscal model.

title: Some States Already Preparing for Potential Supreme Court Ban on Late Ballots
summary: The court’s conservatives appear skeptical of laws allowing mail ballots to arrive after Election Day. A decision could come as late as June, potentially scrambling midterm elections.

title: The Brigade System Helps Restaurants Succeed. Does It Also Lead to Abuse?
summary: Allegations against Noma’s chef have spurred debate over whether a 19th-century model for organizing kitchen staffs breeds physical and psychic violence.

title: Matt Brittin, Former Google Executive, Named the New BBC Head
summary: Matt Brittin, the former president of Google in Europe, will become the new director general. Among the items on his to-do list: handling a lawsuit from President Trump.

title: A Phone-Free Childhood? One Irish Village Is Making It Happen.
summary: Tired of seeing its elementary-school children struggle with online temptations, the town of Greystones proposed a ‘no smart devices’ code. Most everyone bought in.

title: In This Corner of the Internet, Everyone Looks Better Bald
summary: A Reddit forum has become a haven for those deciding whether it’s time to shave it all.

title: There’s a Reason Air Travel Is Such a Mess
summary: There's a deeper story behind the turbulence in the airline industry.

title: For Putin, the War in Iran Changed Everything
summary: In one swoop, it overturned the conditions for conciliation.

title: Airline Anxiety
summary: We look at why flying right now is awful.

title: Tracy Kidder, Author of ‘The Soul of a New Machine,’ Dies at 80
summary: A Pulitzer Prize-winning narrative journalist, he wrote deeply reported books that often focused on heroic goodness in people.

title: Stephen Colbert Is Writing a New ‘Lord of the Rings’ Movie
summary: In an announcement video with the director Peter Jackson, the late-night host said he was developing a new film based on early chapters of the trilogy.

title: Mamdani Is Quietly Backing Away From a Threat to Raise Property Taxes
summary: The mayor has left lawmakers with the belief he does not plan to pursue an unpopular property tax increase, as he looks to close a budget gap of $5.4 billion over two years.

title: Two States Sue Cord Blood Bank Over False Advertisements
summary: The attorneys general of Texas and Arizona contend that Cord Blood Registry, which stores umbilical cord cells, profited from misleading new parents.
